subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Hazmat Awareness for Civilians
description: A short lesson to help you apply Hazmat Awareness for Civilians.

hook.text: A truck overturns on the highway. Diamond-shaped signs on its side tell you what's inside.

buildup.text: HAZMAT placards use colors, numbers, and symbols to communicate chemical dangers.

discovery.text: Red means flammable. Yellow means oxidizer. White means poison. Blue means health hazard.

twist.text: The safest action for civilians is always to move upwind, uphill, and away—never investigate.

climax.text: Calling 911 and describing the placard helps HAZMAT teams prepare before they arrive.

punchline.text: Read the diamond. Move away. Call it in. Leave the rest to the experts.

quiz.question: What should civilians do when encountering a HAZMAT spill?
options: Move upwind and uphill, call 911, describe the placard
Wait for instructions from the driver
